engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru R1 Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the EN07-series engines fitted to the Subaru R1 in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical replacement costs based on current UK market data for grey import specialists.

cards.altText: Subaru R1 EN07D engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil use after 60k miles on high-mileage grey imports.
Timing chain tensioner wear - cold start rattle on units exceeding 70k miles without proper maintenance.
VVT solenoid wear - causes rough idle and warning lights on higher-mileage Japanese imports.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,400-£2,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07 units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru R1 EN07X engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
productionHistory: Subaru R1 STi, STi Aero (JB chassis) - 2008-2010
knownFailures: Supercharger bearing wear - whining noise and boost loss after 50k miles on enthusiast-driven grey imports.
Intercooler hose degradation - boost leaks common on high-mileage units causing reduced performance.
Timing chain tensioner wear - cold start rattle exacerbated by supercharger heat cycles.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,800-£2,600 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07X units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Subaru R1 EN07U engine - petrol 0.66L replacement UK
productionHistory: Subaru R1 Limited, S Low Emission (JB chassis) - 2007-2010
knownFailures: Oil consumption - revised piston rings reduce but do not eliminate oil use on high-mileage units.
Emissions system sensitivity - UK MOT compliance requires proper catalytic converter function.
VVT solenoid wear - causes rough idle and emissions test failures on urban-driven imports.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,500-£2,300 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EN07U units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru R1 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru R1 relies solely on the EN07-series 0.66L petrol engine across all UK grey import variants. While generally reliable for a kei car, supercharger bearing wear on EN07X units, timing chain tensioner rattle, and oil consumption on naturally aspirated EN07D variants dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: EN07X Supercharger Bearing Wear - Why Subaru R1 STi Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Subaru R1 STi, STi Aero (2006-2010, EN07X/EN07Z)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000-70,000 miles on enthusiast-driven grey imports with incomplete Japanese service history
problems.rootCause: Supercharger bearings wear from heat cycles and oil starvation; degraded seals cause whining noise, boost loss, and eventual seizure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, rebuild or replace with OEM-spec unit, oil feed line inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if engine internals undamaged; requires kei car supercharger specialist knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07X Supercharged Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07X with new supercharger, upgraded gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if supercharger failure caused oil contamination or engine damage; peace of mind for STi owners

problems.recommendation: If your R1 STi shows only boost loss with no knocking or oil contamination, supercharger replacement at £550-£950 may suffice. However, if oil contamination occurred or mileage is high, a rebuilt EN07X at £1,800-£2,600 avoids repeat failures and protects your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07X supercharged engine prices for your Subaru R1

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Cold Start Rattle on Subaru R1 EN07 Engines
problems.affectedModels: Subaru R1 all variants (2005-2010, EN07 series)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if oil changes delayed or incorrect viscosity used
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure on cold start; rattle indicates chain slap that can damage guides if ignored, leading to timing jump and valve damage on interference-design kei engines.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, inspect chain and guides, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; prevents secondary damage to sprockets and valves

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07 with Revised Tensioner
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 with updated tensioner, full timing service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattle accompanied by performance loss or high mileage; eliminates cold start noise

problems.recommendation: If your R1 shows only cold-start rattle with no performance loss, tensioner replacement at £280-£480 is sensible. However, on grey imports over 80k miles, upgrading to a rebuilt EN07 with revised components often proves better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for timing-chain-upgraded Subaru R1 engine replacement

problems.h4: EN07D Oil Consumption - Piston Ring Wear on Subaru R1 Naturally Aspirated
problems.affectedModels: Subaru R1 S, Aero, Limited (2005-2010, EN07D/EN07Y/EN07U)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-80,000 miles on high-mileage grey imports with incomplete service history
problems.rootCause: Piston rings wear from thermal cycling and oil quality variations; increased oil consumption causes blue smoke, fouled spark plugs, and potential catalytic converter damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace piston rings, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; requires kei car boxer specialist knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07 with Upgraded Rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 with upgraded piston rings, full service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil consumption severe or mileage exceeds 70k; peace of mind for high-mileage owners

problems.recommendation: If your R1 shows only minor oil consumption with no blue smoke or performance loss, ring replacement at £750-£1,300 may suffice. However, if consumption is severe or mileage is high, a rebuilt EN07 at £1,400-£2,200 avoids repeat failures.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EN07 engine prices for your Subaru R1

problems.h4: i-CVT Compatibility Issues - Drive Plate Mismatch on Subaru R1 Automatic
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage if incorrect engine code fitted or adaptation not performed
problems.rootCause: Incorrect drive plate specification or missing ECU adaptation causes juddering, poor shift quality, and potential transmission damage on i-CVT equipped R1 models.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace drive plate with i-CVT compatible unit, perform ECU adaptation service
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth operation if transmission internals undamaged; requires CVT specialist knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EN07 with i-CVT Compatibility
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EN07 with new i-CVT drive plate, adaptation service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if incorrect engine fitted or adaptation failed; ensures seamless CVT operation

problems.recommendation: If your R1 i-CVT shows only minor judder with no transmission damage, drive plate replacement at £420-£750 may suffice. However, if incorrect engine was fitted or adaptation failed, a rebuilt EN07 with i-CVT compatibility at £1,500-£2,400 ensures proper operation.
problems.cta: Find i-CVT compatible Subaru R1 engine replacements

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru R1
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru R1's EN07-series engines are compact and efficient when maintained, but grey import status, kei car parts scarcity, and known failure patterns mean replacements can be challenging. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand R1 compatibility, chassis codes (JA/JB), supercharger adaptation, and UK compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your R1 back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru R1 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru R1 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru R1 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru R1 production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their EN07 variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Subaru R1 launched in Japan with JA chassis codes and EN07D/EN07X engines across S, Aero and STi trims.
keyChanges: First production year for Subaru R1 grey imports to UK
JA chassis codes introduced for all variants with EN07D/EN07X engine options
Early EN07D calibration with pre-revision piston rings requiring attention on high-mileage units
knownFor: Early EN07D units may exhibit oil consumption; verify piston ring revision status
EN07X supercharger requires diligent oil change intervals to prevent bearing wear
Strong donor parts availability via shared Daihatsu/Toyota kei car platform
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005 Subaru R1 replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru R1 2005 engine replacement • JA chassis EN07 compatibility • Early EN07X supercharger bearing

years.description: The 2008 facelift introduced JB chassis codes with updated EN07 calibration and EN07U low-emission variant for enhanced reliability.
keyChanges: JB chassis codes replaced JA variants with revised mounting points and emissions systems
EN07D revised to EN07Y with improved piston ring design for reduced oil consumption
EN07U low-emission variant introduced on select markets; rare in UK but present on grey imports
mainEngines: EN07D/EN07Y - 0.66L petrol with revised calibration (S, Aero JB)
EN07X/EN07Z - 0.66L petrol supercharged with updated bearing specs (STi JB)
EN07U - 0.66L petrol low emission (Limited JB, rare UK imports)
knownFor: Updated EN07 calibration reduces oil consumption risk on late-production units
Revised supercharger bearings improve EN07X longevity on enthusiast-driven imports
Final production year 2010 units are rarest and most sought-after by kei car collectors
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008 Subaru R1 replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru R1 2008 engine replacement • JB chassis EN07 compatibility • Late-model emissions certification

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Subaru R1 was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Subaru R1 specialists.

faq.h2: Subaru R1 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru R1 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru R1 engine replacement costs range from £500 for Japanese used imports to £2,600 for UK reconditioned EN07X supercharged units, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Supply & fit services add £400-£700 labour.
keyPoints: Japanese used EN07 engines start from £500 with auction grade verification
UK reconditioned EN07D units range £1,400-£2,200 with 12-month warranty
EN07X supercharged variants typically cost £400-£600 more than naturally aspirated
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£700 depending on specialist location and i-CVT complexity
items.cta: Compare Subaru R1 engine replacement quotes from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine code fits my Subaru R1?
items.answer: All Subaru R1 variants use the EN07-series 0.66L petrol engine code, but chassis codes JA (2005-2007) and JB (2008-2010) determine compatibility with your vehicle's mounting points and emissions systems.
keyPoints: EN07 is the dominant engine family code across S, Aero, Limited and STi variants
Verify chassis code JA for pre-facelift or JB for facelift compatibility before ordering
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code confirmation
EN07X supercharged and EN07U low-emission codes require specialist verification for correct replacement matching
items.cta: Verify your Subaru R1 EN07 engine code instantly via DVLA lookup

items.question: Is a reconditioned Subaru R1 engine better than a used Japanese import?
items.answer: Reconditioned EN07 engines offer UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing known failures like supercharger bearing wear, while used Japanese imports provide lower upfront cost but limited warranty and potential compliance documentation gaps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include 12-month UK warranty vs 3-6 months for Japanese imports
Rebuilt EN07 engines feature upgraded piston rings addressing R1 failure patterns
Japanese imports require additional IVA/MOT documentation verification for UK compliance
Budget-conscious owners may prefer imports; long-term owners benefit from reconditioned reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Subaru R1 engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Subaru R1 UK MOT or registration?
items.answer: Replacement engines must meet UK emissions standards and include proper documentation for DVLA registration; EngineMarket specialists supply EN07 units with Euro 4 certification and IVA support for grey import compliance.
keyPoints: All replacement EN07 engines include UK-spec catalytic converter for MOT emissions compliance
Emissions certification documentation provided for DVLA registration updates on grey imports
IVA test support included for R1 vehicles requiring Individual Vehicle Approval
Specialists handle ECU immobiliser adaptation and i-CVT calibration for seamless UK registration transfer
items.cta: Find MOT-compliant Subaru R1 engine replacements with full documentation

items.question: How long does Subaru R1 engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: UK reconditioned EN07 engines typically dispatch within 2 days; Japanese imports require 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day fitting. Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours for in-stock units.
keyPoints: In-stock UK reconditioned units: 2-day supply & fit turnaround typical for EN07 variants
Japanese import engines: 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day specialist fitting with chassis verification
i-CVT and supercharged variants may require additional time for adaptation service and drivetrain compatibility checks
Emergency replacement helpline available for seized engine scenarios with priority dispatch from UK stock
items.cta: Check live Subaru R1 engine stock availability and delivery timelines

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru R1 engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY engine fitting is not recommended for Subaru R1 grey imports due to kei car-specific tools, i-CVT adaptation requirements, supercharger calibration and UK compliance documentation best handled by specialists.
keyPoints: EN07-series engines require specific lifting equipment and timing tools for safe installation
i-CVT variants need ECU adaptation service - specialist tooling essential for smooth operation
IVA/MOT documentation support requires specialist knowledge of grey import compliance procedures
Supply & fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire, risk of errors and warranty validity
items.cta: Book Subaru R1 engine supply & fit with grey import specialist network

items.question: What are the most common Subaru R1 engine problems?
items.answer: EN07X supercharger bearing wear causing whining noise and boost loss, timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start, and oil consumption on high-mileage naturally aspirated EN07D units dominate Subaru R1 replacement enquiries from UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: Supercharger bearing failure affects EN07X/EN07Z variants; rebuilt units include upgraded bearings as standard
Timing chain tensioner wear common on all EN07 variants; revised tensioners available on rebuilt engines
Oil consumption from piston ring wear on NA units; upgraded rings included on rebuilt EN07D/EN07Y
All rebuilt EN07 units include upgraded components addressing these known grey import failure points
items.cta: Compare Subaru R1 engine replacements with failure-proof upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Subaru R1 engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a reconditioned EN07 price (£1,400-£2,200), replacement offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing original failure patterns.
keyPoints: Minor repairs (supercharger rebuild, tensioner replacement) cost £280-£950 and may be viable for low-mileage units
Major failures (seized supercharger, timing chain jump) typically require full replacement for reliability
Rebuilt EN07 engines include 12-month warranty vs limited coverage on repaired original units
Consider vehicle value: R1 grey imports typically worth £1,500-£3,500 in UK market
items.cta: Get instant repair vs replace cost comparison for your Subaru R1 variant

items.question: Does the Subaru R1 engine work with Daihatsu/Toyota donor parts?
items.answer: Yes - the EN07 engine was also fitted to Daihatsu Mira, Move and Toyota bB in other markets, expanding UK parts availability and reducing lead times for grey import R1 owners seeking replacement components.
keyPoints: EN07 cross-compatibility increases donor pool for hard-to-find R1-specific components
Piston rings, tensioners and sensors often available from Daihatsu/Toyota kei car suppliers in UK
Verify part numbers match your R1 chassis code (JA/JB) and variant before ordering
EngineMarket specialists source from cross-platform kei car inventory to reduce wait times
items.cta: Find cross-compatible Subaru R1 EN07 parts via Daihatsu/Toyota donor network
